mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong labor market conditions, indicated by high payroll gains and low unemployment, signal robust corporate earnings potential, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS > 149,000 and UNRATE < 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding corporate capacity utilization, leading to higher earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases but 20-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts indicate elevated consumer demand for durable goods, boosting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases but 5-day SPX returns are negative in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with low interest rates creates uncertainty about future monetary policy, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL > 250 and FEDFUNDS < 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales figures indicate strong consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during periods of monetary tightening.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of instances where RSAFS increases and FEDFUNDS > 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment rate signals tight labor market conditions that may lead to wage inflation, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE decreases > 0.5pp in 1 month.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDP growth signals economic stagnation, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during recessionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth < 1% annualized.
